key_concepts: [url]Make Synty Style Low Poly Character in Blender!^^https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5dTEv6OBOjs[/url]
Learn to make a synty style low poly character in blender

content: Delete everything from the general scene. Get your reference images into position.

section.header: Looping the Front of the Face
content: Synty character models usually have loop around the chin (Image 1). This is where we will start. Add a Plane to the scene, give it an Edge Loop in the y-axis, remove the left vertices and give it a Mirror Modifier in the x-axis with clipping enabled (Image 2). [k]Alt-N,F[/k] to flip the face normals if needed. Move the Plane to the chin area and scale it down (Image 3). The Edge Loop acts to frame the face. In Side View, scale the plane in the y-axis, move it to the chin area and then give it a rotation (Image 4).

image.caption: Image 1 - Synty-style head
Image 2 - Adding a Plane to the scene, bisecting it with an Edge Loop in the x-axis, removing the left vertices and giving it a Mirror Modifier in the x-axis with clipping enabled
Image 3 - Moving the Plane to the chin area and scaling it down
Image 4 - Scaling the plane in the y-axis, moving it to the chin area and giving it a rotation

content: Select the right edge of the plane and extrude it to the edge of the jawline (Image 5). Go into Side View and move it back slightly to corner of the mouth and rotate the vertices slightly (Image 6). In Front View, extrude the edge to the cheekbone (Image 7). In Side View, move it back slightly to line up with the cheekbone from the side and rotate the edge (Image 8).

image.caption: Image 5 - Extruding right edge of the plane to the edge of the jawline
Image 6 - Moving the extrusion back slightly to corner of the mouth and rotating the vertices slightly
Image 7 - Extruding the edge to the cheekbone
Image 8 - Moving the extrusion back slightly to line up with the cheekbone from the side and rotating

content: In Front View, extrude again to the top of the forehead (Image 9) and then rotating the top edge (Image 10). Select the top Edge Loop and adjust it slightly to create a tapering effect in the Front View (Image 11) and Side View (Image 12).

image.caption: Image 9 - Extruding the edge to the top of the forehead
Image 10 - Rotating slightly
Image 11 - Adjusting top Edge Loop slightly to create a tapering effect in the Front View
Image 12 - Adjusting top Edge Loop slightly to create a tapering effect in the Side View

content: Back in Front View, select the 3 inner vertices and extrude them to the center (Image 13). Fill in the bottom face (Image 14). In Side View, adjust the vertices (Image 15).

image.caption: Image 13 - Extruding the 3 inner vertices to the center
Image 14 - Filling in the bottom face
Image 15 - Adjusting the vertices in Side View

content: Select the vertices on the outside and extrude them towards the center of the head, following the jawline (Image 16). Adjust the contours of the line in the Front View (Image 17). From the side, adjust the top vertices to angle down (Image 18). Extrude the vertices out past the ear (Image 19) and then all the way to the back of the head (Image 20).

image.caption: Image 16 -  Selecting the vertices on the outside and extruding them towards the center of the head, following the jawline
Image 17 - Adjusting the contours of the line in the Front View
Image 18 - Adjusting the top vertices to angle down
Image 19 - Extruding the vertices past the ear
Image 20 - Extruding the vertices out all the way to the back of the head

content: In Top View, move the vertices along the x-axis until they meet the in the middle (Image 21). From the side, adjust the vertices to follow the reference image and align the contours of the edges (Image 22). Select the two middle faces at the top and extrude them along the z-axis (Image 23). From the front, move them along the x-axis until they meet in the middle (Image 24).

image.caption: Image 21 - Moving the vertices along the x-axis until they meet the in the middle
Image 22 - Adjusting the vertices to follow the reference image and align the contours of the edges
Image 23 - Selecting the two middle faces at the top and extrude them along the z-axis
Image 24 - Moving the faces along the x-axis until they meet in the middle

content: Add a Loop Cut along the y-axis to the top faces (Image 25). Align the top vertices with the curve of the head (Image 26). Fill in the gap in front with faces (Image 27). Fix the contours of the inner Edge Loop (Image 28).

image.caption: Image 25 - Adding a Loop Cut along the y-axis to the top faces
Image 26 - Aligning the top vertices with the curve of the head
Image 27 - Filling in the gap in front with faces
Image 28 - Fixing the contours of the inner Edge Loop

content: From the front, move that Edge Loop out to round out the skull (Image 29). In Top View, [k]Ctrl-R[/k] to add an Edge Loop at the back of the skull in the z-axis, and then [k]G[/k] to move it out to round out the back (Image 30). Select the vertices in the back and [k]F[/k] to fill in the gaps with faces (Image 31).

image.caption: Image 29 - Moving the Edge Loop out to round out the skull
Image 30 - Adding an Edge Loop at the back of the skull in the z-axis, and moving it out to round out the back
Image 31 - Selecting the vertices in the back and filling in the gaps with faces

content: With the Knife Tool, cut around the ear shape (Image 32). Select the ear faces and [k]E[/k] to extrude them along the x-axis (Image 33). Select the front vertices and Edge Slide them back to the head (Image 34). Adjust the contours of the ear to match the reference image from the front (Image 35) and the side (Image 36).

image.caption: Image 32 - Cut around the ear shape with the Knife Tool
Image 33 - Extruding the ear faces along the x-axis
Image 34 - Edge Sliding the front vertices back to the head
Image 35 - Adjusting the contours of the ear to match the reference image from the front
Image 36 - Adjusting the contours of the ear to match the reference image from the side

content: Rotate around to the bottom and turn off X-Ray Mode ([k]Alt-Z[/k]) in order to see better. Select the two vertices by the chin and back of the skull and create an edge between them ([k]F[/k]) (Image 37). [l]RMB,S[/k] to subdivide the edge and then create a face below the jaw (Image 38). Select the edge in the back and subdivide that, and then create two new faces to close out the head (Image 39). Cut an edge into the face by the front of the jaw (Image 40). In Side view, adjust the edge underneath the jaw to match the reference image (Image 41).

image.caption: Image 37 - Creating an edge between the two vertices by the chin and back of the skull
Image 38 - Subdividing the edge and creating a face below the jaw
Image 39 - Subdividing the edge in the back and creating two new faces to close out the head
Image 40 - Cutting an edge into the face by the front of the jaw
Image 41 - Adjusting the edge underneath the jaw to match the reference image

content: Cut an edge in the upper back of the head and move it out to round out the top of the head (Image 42).

image.caption: Image 42 - Cutting an edge in the upper back of the head and moving it out to round out the top of the head

key_concepts: [url]Make Synty Style Low Poly Character in Blender!^^https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5dTEv6OBOjs[/url]
Learn to make a synty style low poly character in blender

content: Delete everything from the general scene. The final model will be in a T-position, so make sure your reference images match (Image 1).

image.caption: Image 1 - Deleting everything from the general scene and making sure the reference images match the T-position of the final model

content: Add a cube to the scene with a Mirror Modifier in the x-axis and make it a barrel-shape to match the reference image from the front (Image 2) and side (Image 3). [k]G,G[/k] to edge slide the outer edges to give the torso a barrel shape from the top (Image 4). Add a Loop Cut in the x-axis (Image 5) and shape the outer edges to round out the torso more (Image 6).

image.caption: Image 2 - Adding a cube to the scene with a Mirror Modifier in the x-axis and shaping it to match the reference image from the front
Image 3 - Shaping the cube to match the reference image from the side
Image 4 - Edge sliding the outer edges to give the torso a barrel shape from the top
Image 5 - Adding a Loop Cut in the x-axis
Image 6 - Shaping the outer edges to round out the torso more

content: [k]Ctrl-B[/k] to bevel the Loop Cut that was just added on the side (Image 7). Using the knife Tool, cut an edge under the pectoral and then shape it (Image 8).

image.caption: Image 7 - Beveling the Loop Cut that was just added on the side
Image 8 - Using the Knife Tool to cut an edge under the pectoral and then shaping it

content: [k]Numpad7[/k] into Top View, select the top faces, and [k]I,B[/k] to inset them with the boundary on the left (Image 9). Move the inset faces up (Image 10).

image.caption: Image 9 - Going to Top View, selecting the top faces, and insetting them with the boundary on
Image 10 - Moving the inset faces up

content: Shape the chest muscles (Image 11). Add a Loop Cut for the waist, rotate it slightly, and then scale it in the x- (Image 12) and y-axes (Image 13) to match the contours of the reference image. Add another Loop Cut for the waist, rotate, and scale to match the reference image (Image 14). Select the bottom edge loop and rotate slightly to create a curving area for the lower abdominal (Image 15).

image.caption: Image 11 - Shaping the chest muscles
Image 12 - Adding a Loop Cut for the waist, rotating it slightly, and scaling it in the x-axis to match the contours of the reference image
Image 13 - Scaling the Loop Cut in the y-axis to match the contours of the reference image
Image 14 - Adding another Loop Cut for the waist, rotating, and scaling to match the reference image
Image 15 - Selecting the bottom edge loop and rotating it slightly to create a curving area for the lower abdominal

content: Add a Loop Cut at the middle of the chest and scale it out in the y-axis (Image 16). Select the four faces around the shoulder/arm area and extrude them (Image 17).

image.caption: Image 16 - Adding a Loop Cut at the middle of the chest and scaling it out in the y-axis
Image 17 - Selecting the four faces around the shoulder and arm area and extruding them

content: [k]S,X,0[/k] the faces to line them up (Image 18). Dissolve the vertices in tbe middle of the faces in order to make the edge loop for the shoulder and face 6 edges, then use Loop Tools to make the face into a circle (Image 19). Scale the circle down and position it for the shoulder (Image 20).

image.caption: Image 18 - Lining up the faces in the x-axis
Image 19 - Dissolving the vertices in the middle of the faces to make the edge loop for the shoulder 6 edges, then using Loop Tools to make the face into a circle
Image 20 - Scaling the circle down and positioning it for the shoulder

content: Extrude the edge loop out, rotate and scale to form the end of the shoulder (Image 21). Extrude out one more edge loop (Image 22). In Top View, move the shoulders back slightly in the y-axis (Image 23). [k]G,X[/k], [k]S,X,0[/k] to extrude to the elbow (Image 24) and then to the wrist (Image 25). Add an edge loop to the forearm and then [k]S,Z[/k] to shape the arms from the front (Image 26).

Select the edge loop for the elbow and [k]Shift-S,T[/k] to move the 3D Cursor there (Image 27). Change the Transform Pivot Point to 3D Cursor and rotate the forearm (Image 28). Select the loops for the shoulder and scale them up in the y-axis (Image 29). Add a Loop Cut to the middle of the upper arm, rotate, and scale (Image 30).

Add two Loop Cuts around the elbow (Image 31). Merge the vertices along the side in the center on the top and bottom (Image 32). Dissolve the edges along the side (Image 33). Scale in the edges in front and back (Image 34). Move the back edge out to form the elbow (Image 35).

Delete any faces on the bottom (Image 36). Extrude the bottom edge loop down and [k]S,Z,0[/k] (Image 37). Shape the hips from the side (Image 38). Add a face between the faces in front and back (Image 39). Add two edge loops and bring that face down (Image 40). Use LoopTools to make the leg hole a circle, and rotate and scale it into position from the front (Image 41) and side (Image 42).

Add a Loop Cut to the middle of the hips and round them out (Image 43). Extrude the edge loop for the legs down a bit and then down to the ankles (Image 44). Use the Knife Tool to add an edge in front for the crotch (Image 45). Move the front vertex out in the y-axis (Image 46). Move the front vertices down in the z-axis to correct the topology flow around the hips (Image 47). Repeat the same steps in the back (Image 48).

Add two Loop Cuts to the leg for the thickest part of the thighs and the knees and scale them down (Image 49). Add a Loop Cut for the calves and shape the calf and ankle edges from the front (Image 50) and side (Image 51). Add Loop Cuts above and below the knee (Image 52). In the back, edge slide the three vertices above the knee down together in the middle (Image 53). Dissolve the middle vertex (Image 54) and then recreate the faces in the back (Image 55). Dissolve the vertex in the middle below the back of the knee and then re-create those faces (Image 56). In the front, cut a diamond shape around the knee (Image 57). From the side drag the vertex for the knee out (Image 58), and grab the vertices on either side of the knee and [k]G,G[/k] to edge slide them back (Image 59).

Select the bottom edge loop and extrude twice a short distance and then a third time all the way down (use Snap to Grid if you want to go the 0.0 on the z-axis) (Image 60). Select the vertex at the bottom of the ankle in the front and move it up (Image 61). Select the two front faces and extrude them to the toes (Image 62). Edge Slide the front of the foot down (Image 63). Select the bottom ankle loops and [k]S,Y[/k] to match the reference image (Image 64), then scale up the back half of the foot in the y-axis (Image 65). Select the vertices of the foot and [k]R,Z,6[/k] to rotate them outwards (Image 66). Widen the front of the feet (Image 67). Add a Loop Cut for the top of the foot and move it down (Image 68). Straighten up the loops on the bottom and fill in any holes (Image 69).
Review the Hands portion of Markus' tutorial, and use the previous Synty-Style Head tutorial. Attach to the body and rig.
